sources.name: sprites-inc.co.uk — Mega Man Physics Forum
sources.notes: Community physics documentation: jump velocity 4.75 px/f (MM4+), walk 1.375 px/f (claimed), gravity 0.25 px/f², fall cap 7 px/f (MM3-6), MM1-2 fall cap 12 px/f.

sources.name: TASVideos — NES Mega Man series
sources.notes: MM2/3 ROM-disassembly confirmed values: walk $01.80 = 1.5 px/f, gravity $40 = 0.25 px/f², terminal velocity 4.0 px/f. MM4-6 values not separately documented in reviewed sources.

notes: Mega Man 4, 5, and 6 use the same core 8.8 fixed-point physics engine as Mega Man 2 and 3.
PRIMARY ADDITION: Mega Man 4 introduced the Mega Buster charge shot (two charge levels). Present in all three games.
MM3 already has slide — all three games retain it. Slide mechanics appear unchanged from MM2/3.

DISCREPANCY: Walk speed reported by community forum (1.375 px/f) differs from MM2/3 TASVideos ROM data (1.5 px/f = $01.80). The forum claim may be inaccurate or represent MM1/4-specific values. VERIFY.
DISCREPANCY: Terminal velocity forum reports 7 px/f (MM3-6) but TASVideos MM2/3 data shows 4.0 px/f. Large discrepancy — needs ROM verification.
Jump velocity: forum reports 4.75 px/f for MM4+ vs TASVideos MM2: 6.0 px/f, MM3: 5.5 px/f. Each game in the series may have individually tuned jump. VERIFY.
Gravity: 0.25 px/f² confirmed across all sources (consistent with MM2/3 TASVideos data).
MM6 introduced Rush Adaptor — Rush can combine with Mega Man (Power Adaptor, Jet Adaptor). Different from MM3 Rush Jet.

subpixel_system.description: Same engine as NES MM2 and MM3. Velocities are multiples of 16 sub-pixels.

notes: MM2/3 walk speed is ROM-confirmed at $01.80 = 1.5 px/frame from TASVideos disassembly.
Community forum (sprites-inc.co.uk) claims 1.375 px/f for general NES Mega Man. This may be wrong or reflect a different per-game tuning.
1.375 = 352/256 = $01.60 in 8.8. If true, MM4-6 would be slightly slower than MM2/3.
VERIFY: Check ROM for each of MM4/5/6 individually.

ground_movement.notes: Instant velocity, same as all NES Mega Man games.

notes: Forum claims 4.75 px/f for MM4 and later.
This would represent a progressive decrease: MM2 (6.0) → MM3 (5.5) → MM4+ (4.75?).
A 4.75 px/f jump with 0.25 px/f² gravity gives max height ≈ 45px (2.8 tiles). Plausible.
VERIFY: Check MM4/5/6 ROM individually. The trend may be real but the specific values need confirmation.

jump.notes: Variable height via early release — same as MM2/3.

gravity.source: Confirmed across all sources (MM2/3 ROM data, forum, community)
gravity.notes: The 0.25 px/f² gravity constant is consistent across all NES Mega Man games in all sources. This is the series constant.

notes: MAJOR DISCREPANCY: TASVideos MM2/3 data gives terminal velocity 4.0 px/f. Forum claims 7 px/f (MM3-6) and 12 px/f (MM1-2).
A 3x discrepancy in terminal velocity is substantial. One of these sources must be wrong.
Possible explanation: the forum may be confusing sub-pixels and pixels, or using a different definition.
4.0 px/f = 1024 sub-px in 8.8 system (hex: $04.00). 7 px/f = 1792 sub-px ($07.00).
VERIFY: Check ROM data directly for MM4/5/6 terminal velocity values.

slide.notes: Slide introduced in MM2 (first game). Retained in MM3-6 with same or very similar values. No change documented.

mega_buster_charge.description: Holding B charges the Mega Buster. Releasing fires a charged shot.

level_1.description: Partially charged shot — slightly larger, slightly more damage
level_1.visual: yellow flash on Mega Man's arm

level_2.description: Fully charged shot — largest projectile, most damage

notes: The charge shot fundamentally changes the combat rhythm — players can charge while walking/sliding.
Charging and shooting special weapons simultaneously becomes a key optimization in MM4-6.
This is considered a major quality-of-life improvement over MM1-3 where only rapid-fire was possible.

mega_man_4.rush_items: Same as MM3 (Rush Coil, Rush Jet, Rush Marine)

new_mechanics: Rush Adaptor: Power Adaptor (Rush merges with Mega Man — punch attack, break barriers)
Rush Adaptor: Jet Adaptor (Rush merges — fly ability, hovering)
mega_man_6.notes: Rush Adaptor replaces calling Rush separately — Rush becomes wearable armor.

invincibility_frames.notes: Same as MM2/3 — NES Mega Man series standard.

_verification_checklist: Walk speed for each of MM4, MM5, MM6 from ROM (is it 1.5 or 1.375?)
Jump initial velocity for each game (is it 4.75 or closer to MM3's 5.5?)
Terminal velocity for each game (4.0 or 7.0 px/f?)
Charge shot charge time frames for both levels
Rush Adaptor flight speed for MM6
design_notes_for_giants_drink: The Mega Buster charge shot (MM4-6) changes the 'feel' of shooting in the series. Instead of pure rapid-fire, players develop a rhythm of charge-move-release-recharge. This makes the combat feel more deliberate.

The core physics (if walk = 1.5, gravity = 0.25, slide = 3.0) are indistinguishable from MM2/3 to most players. The series' character comes from the level design and robot master design, not from physics differentiation.

MM6's Rush Adaptors are interesting from a paradigm perspective: they change Mega Man's primary movement mode mid-level. Power Adaptor = brawler; Jet Adaptor = flier. Same character, different movement vocabulary.

The progressive jump height decrease (MM2: 6.0 → MM3: 5.5 → possibly lower in MM4+) suggests Capcom was tuning difficulty via jump capability. Shorter jump = harder to avoid lower-trajectory attacks and harder to access elevated platforms.
